oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Parrots and Palms
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Waugh, David
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Whenever you look at photos or videos of perched parrots, there is a good chance they will be perched in a palm tree. Food, especially palm fruits, is a strong reason for that association. This article discusses research on parrot-palm interactions, including the ways parrots may act not only as seed predators but also as seed dispersers and ecological partners. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): AFA Watchbird April 2025
